derivation: midpoint of the empty gap between the pass and dead incumbent populations (deployed convention: certify.probes.grade_splices tau_copy)
authority: RUNBOOK §0 — M1c is 'hub-collapsed (polygon column = sink artifact)'; M1b is merely weak ('discrimination only inside camera-tagged strata')

thresholds.form:
FAIL if ANY of the three trips (OR-structured — the instruments see
different failures: the prediction column is a k=1 phenomenon, so rank-2..10
absorption casts no 1-NN votes and is invisible to it, which is how the
incumbent m1b decouples). Terminal regardless of accuracy (§1.4). Applies to
EVERY candidate space/descriptor — motion and appearance alike (§7 cond. 3).
thresholds.coverage_interaction:
Computed on the same matrix the exam grades, over the DEFINED SUBPOOL only,
denominators explicit, NO coverage correction or renormalization (that would
be a new bar form). The §3.2/§3.3 definedness gates are SUPPOSED to remove
near-static clips, and a shrunken pool mechanically concentrates hub
statistics — that is a property of the candidate, not an artifact to correct
away. Coverage is reported beside every hubness stat.
thresholds.band_disclosure:
A candidate statistic landing INSIDE the band gets the frozen verdict —
terminal, no re-adjudication by the executor — but the report must flag
"landed in the calibration band; verdict is threshold-placement-sensitive"
and print the band and both populations. Whether the placement was right is
owner-side review.
